prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='catboost', seasonality_champion='ONLY_DWT_catboost', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.93) and high seasonality variance (1.25), with catboost leading trend tracking while ONLY_DWT_catboost excels at capturing seasonal patterns. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, supporting per-horizon selection methods. Given the high disagreement and variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths without over-relying on any single approach.

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "ONLY_DWT_catboost",
  "overall_champion": "ARIMA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_DWT_rf", "late": "ONLY_CWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["ARIMA", "THETA", "ETS", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "FT_rf", "NaiveSeasonal", "CWT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["CWT_rf", "catboost", "FT_svr", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.9283,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.93) and high seasonality variance (1.25), with catboost leading trend tracking while ONLY_DWT_catboost excels at capturing seasonal patterns. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, supporting per-horizon selection methods. Given the high disagreement and variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths without over-relying on any single approach."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "ridge_stacking_l250_topk3"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=10 indicates high model diversity per horizon, favoring selection methods like best_per_horizon and topk_mean. RMSE spread_ratio=1.93 from pattern_analyst_insights suggests dba_combination is recommended for handling phase shifts. With n_windows=3 (very few), small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.25 provides stability. ridge_stacking adds non-linear meta-learning. Pattern analyst explicitly recommends dba_combination due to high seasonality_variance=1.25 and model disagreement."
}

Given the high disagreement and variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths without over-relying on any single approach.
